summary: Aggregation of the five specialist reviews for Part IV ('From Lattice Models to Effective Field Theories: Stabilization and the Invertible Condensed Phase Spectrum'). The panel is highly consistent: the paper is technically mostly_sound (technical_correctness 0.86; 0 critical, 0 major, 2 minor framing issues across 12 claims), reproducible (0.88; deterministic byte-identical build, 21/21 QuickCheck properties passing, MIT-licensed in-repo code), and honestly cited (33/33 references real and resolvable, no fabrication or hallucination). Its defining strength, echoed by every layer including the external Gemini review (ACCEPT after three rounds), is a rigorous separation of theorem from conjecture: the central lattice-to-EFT agreement is stated only as the open three-level Conjecture IV-2 (L0 phase sets / L1 spectra / L2 families over condensed probes), with genuine (L0) theorems supplied only in d=1 (Ogata's split-property index) and for free fermions (the tenfold way, all 80 table entries machine-checked). Novelty is modest but well-calibrated by the authors' own admission (0.35; the sole apparently-unprecedented contribution is the explicit L0/L1/L2 stratification of the comparison question). The Codex NEEDS_FIX verdict is environmental only (a read-only sandbox could not rebuild the PDF) and the fresh build was independently verified by the worker. No finding rises to major or critical. The panel identifies four must-fix (blocking) items before publication -- two correctness/framing rewordings (conditionalize the def:comp comparison-map 'Definition' on Conjecture I-1 and Freed-naturality; soften the overstated 2D Ogata 'completeness' claim) and two citation-integrity fixes (add the named-but-uncited Altland-Zirnbauer 1997 reference; cite Ogata's primary-source theorem paper arXiv:2110.04671 for the d=1 completeness claim rather than the non-stating ICM survey arXiv:2110.04675) -- all trivially addressable and consistent with a minor revision. Eleven further advisory items (novelty citation-positioning, reproducibility packaging/documentation, and residual cosmetic nits) are recommended but non-gating. Note: the consolidated fix list has already been dispatched to the authoring worker; this file is the panel's aggregation of record and the meta-reviewer made no edits to the paper.
strengths: Exemplary theorem/conjecture discipline: the central lattice-to-EFT agreement is confined to the open three-level Conjecture IV-2 and never claimed proven, and all five extrapolations (IV-1..IV-5) carry stable Conjecture identifiers rather than being upgraded to theorems (technical_correctness TC12; Gemini praises the 'rigid line' between theorem and conjecture).

Core algebra is rigorous and independently machine-checked: the Grothendieck group-completion universal property with the explicit '+e' stabilization witness, the commutative-monoid stacking laws, and the corrected non-cancellative toy example K(M) = Z generated by [t] are all verified, with 21 QuickCheck properties passing reproducibly (technical_correctness TC1-TC3; reproducibility 0.88).

Tenfold-way evidence is exhaustively correct: all 80 Table-1 entries recomputed from G(s,d) = d_bullet((s-d) mod p) and matched, with landmark phases (Kitaev chain, p+ip, quantum spin Hall, IQHE, SSH) independently checked against known physics and the Bott periodicities machine-verified (technical_correctness TC6, TC11).

Citation surface is honest and fully resolvable: all 33 \cite keys resolve, no fabricated or hallucinated references, and eleven load-bearing attributions (Freed-Hopkins, Kubota's Omega-spectrum including its crystallographic variant, Kapustin-Sopenko, Kitaev, the Clausen-Scholze 2605.xxxxx postings) were independently web-verified (citation review).

Reproducible, openly-licensed artifact: deterministic build, MIT-licensed sources, reproducibility 0.88 with only low-severity packaging gaps; the external Gemini review returned ACCEPT and the Codex NEEDS_FIX was confirmed environmental-only (read-only sandbox) with a fresh build verified by the worker.

Novelty is honestly calibrated (0.35): the paper attributes its algebraic machinery to standard mathematics, explicitly flags the condensed refinement of its own central object as Conjecture IV-1, and the one genuinely new contribution -- the explicit (L0)/(L1)/(L2) comparison stratification -- appears unprecedented in the prior literature (novelty review).
weaknesses: [BLOCKING] Comparison map presented as an unconditional Definition (technical_correctness TC7, def:comp). The existence of c_{d,G} as a map of spectra rests on Conjecture I-1 (for IP^cond to be a spectrum at all) and on an asserted-not-constructed naturality of Freed's SRE-to-TQFT assignment, yet def:comp reads as a rigorous construction a reader could mistake for unconditional. Fix: reword so the map's existence is part of the hypotheses of Conjecture IV-2 (e.g. 'Assuming IP^cond is a spectrum, there is a comparison map ...').

[BLOCKING] 2D Ogata 'completeness' overstated (technical_correctness TC8, discussion following thm:d1). The parenthetical claim that Ogata's H^3(G,T) index yields the (L0) comparison in d=2 asserts a completeness result stronger than the cited literature supports. Fix: state that Ogata constructs an H^3(G,T)-valued index in d=2 (a well-defined comparison homomorphism at pi_0) without asserting it is a complete invariant, or add a caveat that 2D completeness is not known.

[BLOCKING] Named foundational reference uncited (citation, def:tenfold). Definition 5.1 names 'the ten Altland-Zirnbauer symmetry classes' verbatim, but neither the paper nor references.bib cites Altland & Zirnbauer (Phys. Rev. B 55, 1142, 1997); only Kitaev's 2009 repackaging is cited. Fix: add the Altland-Zirnbauer bibliography entry at the point the classes are named.

[BLOCKING] Load-bearing theorem cited to a non-stating survey (citation, thm:d1). The d=1 completeness claim is sourced to Ogata's terse ICM proceedings survey (arXiv:2110.04675), whose own abstract does not state the specific H^2(G,T)-completeness theorem; the precise primary source arXiv:2110.04671 is absent from the bibliography. Fix: cite arXiv:2110.04671 (the primary-source theorem paper) in addition to or instead of the survey.

[ADVISORY] Uncited direct precedent for the stacking monoid (novelty, prop:monoid / thm:groth). Xiong (arXiv:1701.00004) already develops the stacking-as-commutative-monoid picture with invertibles exactly the SRE phases; it is cited elsewhere for the generalized-cohomology hypothesis but not at the point of the closely analogous construction, understating existing physics precedent. Fix: cross-reference Xiong at Prop 'monoid'/Thm 'groth'.

[ADVISORY] Fragile-topology literature uncited (novelty, rem:stmeaning / ex:noncanc). The 'stabilization collapses distinctions' phenomenon the paper isolates closely parallels the named 'fragile topological phases' literature (Po, Vishwanath, Watanabe, e.g. arXiv:1709.06551), which the Discussion echoes without citing. Fix: cite the fragile-topology source(s).

[ADVISORY] Standard stacking-group SPT precedents uncited (novelty, Section 4). The abelian-group-under-stable-equivalence picture is standard SPT lore (Chen-Gu-Liu-Wen-style stable-equivalence classifications; explicit fermionic SPT stacking-group treatments, e.g. Phys. Rev. B 110, 235117 (2024)) and is not cited where the construction is presented. Fix: add representative citations at the construction point.

[ADVISORY] Free-fermion Grothendieck-vs-K-theory subtlety unaddressed (novelty, prop:stable / Sec. 6.2). Naive Grothendieck completion of the free-fermion stacking monoid does not always recover the correct topological K-theory classification group; leaving this unaddressed bounds how much weight the free-fermion 'evidence' can carry for Conjecture IV-2. Fix: acknowledge and cite the subtlety.

[ADVISORY] Orphan-instance test-suite hygiene under strict flags (reproducibility). Under the review brief's -Wall -Wextra -Werror the test:properties component fails to compile (three orphan Arbitrary instances at Properties.hs:30/36/39; -Worphans is already on under -Wall alone), while the executable target and the project's own -Wall configuration build clean and all 21 properties pass. Test-only, meets the repo's own stated config. Fix: add {-# OPTIONS_GHC -Wno-orphans #-} to Properties.hs or relocate the instances.

[ADVISORY] Toolchain pin undocumented (reproducibility). cabal.project pins with-compiler ghc-9.10.1, which is not on PATH by default on the test machine (ghcup-only) and produces an opaque 'Cannot find the program ghc' failure on a first from-scratch attempt; src/lattice-eft-equivalence/ has no README or build instructions. Fix: add brief build instructions noting the pinned GHC (e.g. 'ghcup install ghc 9.10.1').

[ADVISORY] No dependency freeze or version bounds (reproducibility). There is no cabal.project.freeze and no bounds beyond base >=4 && <5 for containers or QuickCheck, so the exact resolved package versions a from-scratch clone would pick are unrecorded. Fix: commit a cabal.project.freeze or add explicit version bounds.

[ADVISORY] No external code archive for PDF-only readers (reproducibility). No repository URL, DOI, or Zenodo-style archive appears in the manuscript or bibliography, so a reader holding only the PDF cannot fetch the (in-repo, MIT-licensed) code. Fix: add a code-availability URL or DOI to the manuscript.

[ADVISORY] Periodic-table co-origin citation omitted (citation, def:tenfold). Schnyder-Ryu-Furusaki-Ludwig (Phys. Rev. B 78, 195125, 2008) co-originates the tenfold-way table and is commonly cited jointly with Kitaev; it is absent. Lower priority, since Kitaev alone is a defensible single citation. Fix: optionally add Schnyder et al. alongside Kitaev.

[ADVISORY] Forward reference to a not-yet-written Part VI (citation, paperSynthesis). paperSynthesis (Part VI) is cited but, unlike its four sibling companion papers that already exist as compiled .tex/.pdf, has no corresponding manuscript yet per the project task tracker. Standard practice for a coordinated modular release; flagged only for reconciliation. Fix: ensure Part VI exists and is consistent with what IV-2 is described as feeding into before the series is finalized.

[ADVISORY] Residual presentation nits (Gemini round 3). Remark 2.4's 'boxed slogan' is rendered with \emph and has no visual box (box it or reword to 'italicized slogan'), and Conjecture IV-3's restriction notation nu|_{U_f} is redundant since nu_f is already defined on U_f. Both cosmetic. Fix: apply the two wording/formatting tweaks.
questions: Will the def:comp fix restructure Conjecture IV-2 so the existence of the comparison map is explicitly contingent on Conjecture I-1 (IP^cond being a spectrum) and on the naturality of Freed's SRE-to-TQFT assignment, rather than being asserted as an unconditional Definition?

For d=2, will the authors downgrade the H^3(G,T) 'completeness' language to a comparison homomorphism at pi_0, or can they point to a result establishing genuine 2D completeness that supports the stronger claim?

How much of Section 4's stacking / group-completion construction is intended as novel versus a condensed repackaging of existing SPT stacking-group results, and does the known failure of naive Grothendieck completion to recover topological K-theory for free fermions limit the weight the free-fermion evidence carries for Conjecture IV-2?
Will Part VI (paperSynthesis) be completed and reconciled with the role Conjecture IV-2 is described as playing in it before the series is finalized?
